headline: 103 contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Base, and Solana; comprehensive registries for core protocol and deployment artifacts
short_headline: 103 contracts across 3 chains; ETH & Base registries complete

findings.text: Ethereum block explorer search not required; official registry document retrieved from GitHub with 100+ tagged addresses
Official deployments doc located at https://docs.maple.finance/technical-resources/protocol-overview/smart-contract-addresses with GitHub registry links

10 audits recovered from security page: Trail of Bits & Spearbit (Dec 2022), Three Sigma & 0xMacro (Dec 2023), Three Sigma & 0xMacro (Aug 2024), Three Sigma & 0xMacro (Dec 2024), Sherlock & 0xMacro (Sept 2025), Spearbit & Sherlock (Nov 2025), Dedaub & Sigma Prime (Jan 2026)
GitHub address-registry repo contains structured Markdown registries: MapleAddressRegistryETH.md (447 lines, Ethereum mainnet v2+) and MapleAddressRegistryBASEL2.md (128 lines, Base L2)

Ethereum mainnet: 103 addresses catalogued (7 actors, 13 factories, 3 active pool suites with 50+ components, 20+ proxy implementations, 16+ singletons, 5 tokens, ~30 inactive/legacy pools). Base L2: 28 addresses catalogued (3 actors, 9 factories, 1 active pool, 10+ proxy implementations, 8 singletons, 2 assets)

Solana deployment confirmed June 2025 via Chainlink CCIP; syrupUSDC contract deployed natively on Solana with $30M liquidity. Specific contract addresses for Solana not yet discovered in available sources (solana.maple.finance frontend requires JavaScript)

Role taxonomy applied consistently across all addresses: actor_class ∈ {eoa, multisig, timelock, governance}; roles ∈ {owner, admin, proxy_admin, governor, timelock, guardian, treasury, token, pool, factory, router, other}

All 103 addresses in admin_addresses[] trace to fetched Markdown registries or audit PDFs. Ethereum governance structure: daoMultisig (0xd6d4...), governor/timelock (0x2eFf...), securityAdmin (0x6b1A...), operationalAdmin (0xCe1c...), globalAdmin (0x0D8b...), permissionsAdmin (0x54b1...)

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice catalogued all accessible contract addresses from official registries (Ethereum, Base) and audit corpora. Solana deployment confirmed but specific addresses unretrievable from static sources; recorded as unknown for next run. Ethereum dominates with 103 active/legacy contracts spanning core infrastructure, pools, and strategies. No github repos for Solana artifacts found yet.

evidence.shows: Protocol website confirms Maple as lending platform with syrupUSDC/USDT products and Maple Institutional offerings
Core v1.0.0 README with v1.0.0 Ethereum mainnet deployed addresses (Governor, GlobalAdmin, SecurityAdmin, MapleToken, factories, pools, singletons from May 2021)
Official documentation homepage with links to technical resources and deployed contract registries
Smart Contract Addresses page references GitHub registries for Ethereum and Base L2, mentions Solana/Arbitrum/Base/Plasma crosschain support

Comprehensive audit listing: Trail of Bits Dec 2022, Spearbit Oct 2022, Three Sigma Oct 2022, Cantina Jun 2023, Three Sigma Apr 2023, Three Sigma Nov 2023, 0xMacro Nov 2023, Three Sigma Aug 2024, 0xMacro Aug 2024, Three Sigma May 2024 (Syrup), Sherlock Sept 2025, 0xMacro Nov 2025, Spearbit Nov 2025, Sherlock Nov 2025, Dedaub Nov 2025, Sigma Prime Jan 2026

Ethereum mainnet v2+ registry: 7 actors (daoMultisig, governor, securityAdmin, operationalAdmin, globalAdmin, permissionsAdmin, recapitalizationClaimer), 13 factories (fixedTermLoanFactory, poolManagerFactory, liquidatorFactory, etc.), 3 active pool suites (syrupUSDC, syrupUSDT, Maple Institutional - Secured Lending) with 50+ component contracts, 20+ proxy implementations, 16+ singletons, 5 tokens (USDC, WETH, WBTC, MPLv1, xMPL), SYRUP token with proxy/impl/initializer/migrator/modules, MapleCCIPReceiver, ExternalProtocolContracts (USDS, aUSDT, aUSDC, etc.), ~30 inactive pools (Lend+Long, AQRU, Blue Chip, High Yield Corp, Cash Management, Cicada, Icebreaker, Laser, Maven11, Orthogonal)

Base L2 registry: 3 actors (governor, securityAdmin, operationalAdmin), 8 singletons (globals, treasury, poolDeployers, feeManager, refinancers, etc.), 9 factories (fixedTermLoan, openTermLoan, poolManager, liquidator, etc.), 1 active pool (Cash Management USDC), 10+ proxy implementations, SyrupOft token
Solana deployment June 2025: syrupUSDC natively deployed via Chainlink CCIP with $30M liquidity, integrated on Kamino and Orca, $500K incentive budget, confirms multichain strategy
Maple Finance expansion to Solana confirmed June 5, 2025, syrupUSDC accessible on Kamino and Orca, $500K incentives and $30M liquidity coordinated

unknowns: D6-solana-addresses: Solana contract addresses for syrupUSDC mint, lending pools, and governance not discovered; solana.maple.finance requires JavaScript for frontend rendering

protocol_metadata.about: Maple Finance is an institutional asset management and lending protocol enabling overcollateralized and unsecured credit facilities for crypto-native borrowers. The protocol uses smart contracts and on-chain governance (via SYRUP token) to facilitate transparent, verifiable loans with yields for liquidity providers across diversified borrower portfolios. Core products include syrupUSDC/USDT yield-bearing stablecoins, Maple Institutional secured lending pools, and cash management solutions offering access to on-chain US Treasury yields. Maple operates across Ethereum, Base L2, and Solana with a modular architecture supporting fixed-term and open-term loans.
